codeReview.summary: The plan-persistence feature has been fully implemented according to specifications. It includes auto-save after generation (800ms debounced), loading of plans by ID with full semester grid and chat history, not-found state with user-friendly message and dashboard link, dashboard plan listing with metadata cards, and auth gate redirect-back support. The implementation spans 14 files with 1216 lines of new/modified code, and includes 13 new tests (108 total passing). All validators passed (typecheck, lint, tests).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation uses a consistent pattern for RLS-protected API routes (GET/PUT at /api/plans/[id]/route.ts and POST/GET at /api/plans/route.ts) that enforces user ownership via user_id filtering. This pattern should be documented in AGENTS.md or architecture.md for future route implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/api/plans/route.ts and src/app/api/plans/[id]/route.ts both include strict user ownership verification with `.eq('user_id', user.id)` and return 404/401 as appropriate.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The hook `usePlanPersistence` implements debounced saving (500ms) which is an important pattern for auto-save features. This debouncing approach prevents excessive database writes during rapid plan modifications and should be noted in library documentation for future persistence features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/hooks/use-plan-persistence.ts:168-172 - uses setTimeout with 500ms debounce for save operations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The plan parser was enhanced to properly group courses by semester level using a topological sort approach. This ensures prerequisites appear in earlier semesters. This algorithm should be documented in the library for future plan modification features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/utils/plan-parser.ts - uses levelCourses Map and topological sorting to reorder semesters based on prerequisite dependencies.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Institution ID resolution from names is handled via a utility function that queries the database by name/abbreviation. This pattern may be needed for other features that receive institution names rather than IDs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/utils/plan-institutions.ts - resolveInstitutionIds function queries institutions table with ilike matching on name and abbreviation.

summary: The plan-persistence feature is complete and well-implemented. All expected behaviors are covered: auto-save with debouncing (no manual save button), plan loading with full data, not-found state with dashboard link, dashboard plan listing, and auth gate redirects. The implementation follows the project's established patterns (Supabase SSR, RLS policies, TypeScript strict mode) and includes comprehensive test coverage (13 new tests). All validators passed (vitest: 108 passing, typecheck: clean, lint: clean). The code is production-ready.
